subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Two Stories About Flying
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

Passages.paragraph: In the first story, a young boy named Fredrick fears heights. His father encourages him to face his fear and climb a hill. Fredrick experiences both fear and excitement as he reaches the top.
questions: Who is Fredrick and what fear does he have?
How does his father help him confront the fear?
What emotions does Fredrick experience?
Identify words that show fear and excitement.
How does this paragraph set the scene for courage?

Passages.paragraph: The second story follows Richard Bach, who learns to fly. He practices with patience and determination. The thrill of flying gives him a sense of freedom and achievement.
questions: Who is Richard Bach and what skill does he learn?
How does he practice and improve?
What feelings does flying give him?
Which words show his determination?
How does this paragraph highlight the theme of perseverance?

Passages.paragraph: Both stories highlight human courage and the joy of overcoming fears. The lessons show that facing challenges leads to personal growth and self-confidence.
questions: What is the common theme in both stories?
How is courage portrayed in the stories?
What lesson do the stories teach?
Which words indicate personal growth?
Why is overcoming fear important in these stories?

Passages.paragraph: Fredrick’s story shows that confronting fears gradually can lead to confidence. The support of family and guidance helps individuals overcome difficult situations.
questions: What does Fredrick’s story teach about facing fears?
How does family support help him?
Which words show his growing confidence?
Why is gradual practice important?
How does this paragraph connect to the theme of courage?

Passages.paragraph: Richard Bach’s story emphasizes self-discipline and persistence. Learning to fly requires patience and repeated effort. The joy of achievement makes the challenges worthwhile.
questions: What is emphasized in Richard Bach’s story?
How does persistence play a role?
Which words show the importance of patience?
What makes the challenges worthwhile?
How does this paragraph reinforce the theme of perseverance?
